title: SQL Joins and Relationships Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about relationships and joins in a relational database.

questions.question: What is the primary role of a 'Join' in SQL?
options: To delete multiple rows at once.
To combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column between them.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: Which of these joins returns rows ONLY where there is a match in BOTH tables?
options: LEFT JOIN
RIGHT JOIN
INNER JOIN
FULL JOIN

questions.question: What is a 'LEFT JOIN' useful for?
options: To find all users and their posts, including users who have NOT posted anything.
To only find users who have exactly 10 posts.
A way to encrypt data before deleting it.
None of the above.

questions.question: In which type of relationship do you typically need a 'Junction Table' (Join Table)?
options: One-to-One
One-to-Many
Many-to-Many
None of the above.

questions.question: What is a 'Foreign Key' in a SQL table?
options: A key that is used to encrypt the entire database.
A column that creates a link between data in two different tables.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.
